---
title: '4,222 Introduction to Programming'
subtitle: 'Lecture 2: Summary'
author: "Dr. Franziska Bender, Dr. Aurélien Sallin"
date: "2026-02-20"
code-line-numbers: false
format:
  revealjs:
    template-partials:
      - ../../assets/style/title-slide.html
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    smaller: true
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
    slide-number: c
    theme: ../../assets/style/hsgtheme.scss
highlight-style: dracula
cache: false
echo: false
---

## Summary: Key Concepts

#### The Terminal

- A text-based interface to interact with your computer
- Mac uses **bash/zsh** (Terminal), Windows uses **Command Prompt** or **PowerShell**

<table style="width:95%; font-size:0.7em;">
  <thead>
    <tr><th>Action</th><th>bash (Mac)</th><th>Command Prompt (Windows)</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><td>Show current directory</td><td><code>pwd</code></td><td><code>cd</code> (without arguments)</td></tr>
    <tr><td>List files</td><td><code>ls</code></td><td><code>dir</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Navigate</td><td><code>cd &lt;path&gt;</code></td><td><code>cd &lt;path&gt;</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Create directory</td><td><code>mkdir</code></td><td><code>mkdir</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Home shortcut</td><td><code>~</code> (e.g. <code>cd ~/Documents</code>)</td><td>No <code>~</code> — use <code></code></td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

- Paths: **absolute** (`C:\Users\Bilbo\Documents\`) vs **relative** (`../Documents/`)
- Shortcuts: `.` (current directory), `..` (parent directory)

## Using Visual Code

#### The IDE

- **VS Code** is our IDE: code editor + terminal + extensions in one tool
- Always open VS Code **from your project directory** (`code .`)
- Install extensions: Python, Pylance, Ruff, Jupyter


## Python & Environments

#### Why Python?

- Readable, versatile, widely used in data science, economics, and AI
- Coming from R: watch out for **0-based indexing** and **indentation**


## Python & Environments

#### Virtual Environments with `uv`

- Each project gets its own **isolated environment** (its own "kitchen")
- Never install packages globally — always use environments!
- Key commands:

<table style="width:80%; font-size:0.8em;">
  <tbody>
    <tr><td><code>uv init --python 3.14.1</code></td><td>Create a new project</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>uv add pandas numpy</code></td><td>Add packages</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>uv run main.py</code></td><td>Run a script in the environment</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>uv sync</code></td><td>Install dependencies from lock file</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

- Key files: `.python-version`, `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `.venv/`
